rationales.R1: §1 invokes additive/multiplicative duality, inverse-function pairs, Euler-derived complex extension, and algebraic-vs-transcendental split without opaque citations.
rationales.R2: §2 gives 8 ordered steps with named identities; pow, log_x, arcsin, arctan derivations all written inline, not skipped.
rationales.R3: §3 exhibits strictly decreasing Stage A(~12) > B(6) > C(5) > D(4), each with a 'why push further' justification.
rationales.R4: §4 states basis, signatures, principal-branch convention; essentiality uses entireness (drop-ln), surjection (drop-exp), arity (drop-+), R>0 closure (drop -1); alternate {+,exp,ln,i} given.
rationales.R5: §5 provides derived toolkit plus a primitive-by-primitive formula table; every formula expressible as {+, exp, ln, -1} composition.
rationales.R5_prime: task/sim/ directory exists but is empty — no verify.py, no output.txt, no executable numeric oracle was produced.
rationales.R6: §6 gives 3 DAG-chain examples (×, cos via Euler, arctan); macros defined that unfold to basis primitives only.
rationales.R7: §4 and §7 note x,y are argument slots not primitives, but the 'one primitive = one constant or one operation symbol' convention is implicit; alternative conventions mentioned but not ranked against each other.
rationales.R8: §7 branch cuts per formula, domain restrictions explicit, real-vs-complex sharpened to concrete open question on closed real sin formula, cardinality-vs-depth and global 3-element lower bound disclosed as open.
rationales.R9: §5 derived toolkit gives dependency order -1 → 1 → 2 → 1/2 → i → π → e, each step using only prior constants.
rationales.R10: iter-0-baseline.md, iter-1-candidate.md, iter-1-accepted.md and rubric-v2.md persisted in task/iterations/; evaluator-report content within iter files not inspected here, so scored conservatively.

gaps.description: No executable numeric oracle exists. task/sim/ is empty — no verify.py that samples each §5 formula at pseudo-random complex points (fixed seed) against numpy/cmath reference, and no output.txt capture.
gaps.suggested_close_action: Add task/sim/verify.py using numpy with a fixed RNG seed, evaluating every §5 primitive formula at ~50 complex sample points, comparing to numpy/cmath reference within tolerance (e.g., 1e-10), printing per-primitive PASS/FAIL and a summary; write stdout to task/sim/output.txt. Cover all §5 primitives (>30 entries).

gaps.description: Counting convention is implicit. The rubric asks for an explicit statement like 'one primitive = one constant or one operation symbol; variables are argument slots'. Alternative conventions (e.g., variables counted, binary +/unary exp collapsed, constants folded) are not enumerated and ranked.
gaps.suggested_close_action: Add a short paragraph at the top of §4 stating the exact counting convention, then list 2–3 alternative conventions with the minimum they yield and a one-sentence ranking justification.

gaps.description: Iteration files exist but the rubric asks each to carry an evaluator report naming closed gaps with §-locations; this cannot be affirmed to rigorous level without inspection of prior iter files (disallowed for this evaluator).
gaps.suggested_close_action: Ensure each new iteration file includes a top-block evaluator report section that enumerates closed gaps from the prior iteration with exact §-locations of the fix.

summary: Strong v1 baseline holds under v2 on dimensions R1–R6, R8, R9; R5' numeric oracle absent (sim/ empty) is the dominant gap; R7 convention and R10 trace discipline minor.
